We enjoyed learning about the history of the Fort and to having viewing the Golden Gate Bridge from underneath.

Very good option if the weather is rainy, like today. Free entry. Ideal photo opportunity for GGB. Very useful and educational, including a 29 min video on the construction of the bridge. Would recommend.

Fort Point is a 19th Century fort that guards the entrance to San Francisco Harbor that replaced an earlier Spanish fort. By the time the Golden Gate Bridge was constructed, it was obsolete, but maintained for its historic value. It has been well-maintained by the NPS with most of the original structure in place. There are several rooms recreating life at the Fort, and a small museum explaining its history. The Fort is literally under the Golden Gate Bridge and provides terrific bridge and San Francisco views from its top level.
